title: Order of Dai Bendu

{{Quote|And so the Dai Bendu waited, they contemplated and meditated and listened with their minds and hearts, as well as their senses.|[[Ketu]]|<ref name="DOJ1" />}}
The '''Order of Dai Bendu''', also known as the '''Bendu''', was one of the many ancient and semi-legendary organizations that studied [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and the [[Midi-chlorian/Legends|midi-chlorians]] in the [[Pre-Republic era]], prior to the birth of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

In [[36,453 BBY]], a [[Tho Yor]] brought a group of Bendu monks from the [[Andobi Mountains/Legends|Andobi Mountains]] of the snowy planet [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]] to the planet [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], where they encountered other [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] groups and took part in the establishment of the [[Je'daii Order]]. A number of elements and symbols borrowed from the Dai Bendu made their way into the Je'daii Order and its successor, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].

Although the Order of Dai Bendu still existed in the waning [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the Republic, its tenets were poorly understood and its involvement with the birth of the Je'daii Order had been mostly forgotten, even in [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] circles. Some [[Historian/Legends|historians]] of that [[Time/Legends|time]] theorized that the Bendu monks were the original incarnation of the Jedi, but lacked solid proofs to back up their claim. Only a few individuals, including the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]], possessed texts pertaining to the ancient Dai Bendu.

==History==
===Early history===
{{Quote|Before the Jedi Order, such Force-sensitive beings were isolated instead of united. Some were regarded as wizards. Others as demons.|[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], on the ancient groups that became the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]|<ref name="WDM">''[[The Wrath of Darth Maul]]''</ref>}}
[[File:TheMidRim.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The Mid Rim, cradle of the Dai Bendu]]
The Dai Bendu monks were active by the 38th millennia before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] on [[Ando Prime/Legends|Ando Prime]], a snowy planet<ref name="DOJ1" /> in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] region of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]],<ref name="TEA" /> where a [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] was located.<ref>{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Power of the Jedi|pack=Obi-Wan Kenobi (Cold Weather Gear)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/potjobiwancold.asp}}</ref> They made their home in the [[Andobi Mountains/Legends|Andobi Mountains]]. All the Bendu monks were [[Talid/Legends|Talids]],<ref name="DOJ1" /> a nomadic species indigenous to Ando Prime.<ref name="S">{{GamerCite|2|Snow Job}}</ref> Talids were often collectively referred to as the "Bendu Tribe,"<ref name="OPG" /> and the term "Dai Bendu" itself was sometimes used to describe a member of the Talid species.<ref name="BAS">In the dramatis persona of ''[[Dawn of the Jedi: Into the Void]]'' (as shown in the book preview on Amazon.com), the novel's characters are described by their [[Species/Legends|species]] and [[Gender/Legends|gender]], and the [[Talid/Legends|Talid]] monk [[Lha-Mi]] is called a "Dai Bendu male."</ref>

At first, the Order of Dai Bendu consisted of peaceful monks dedicated to the study of numerology<ref name="AOTCICS">''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref> and holding to non-confrontational principles.<ref name="OPG">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer Official Nintendo Player's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'' Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]</ref> They were one of the first groups to discover and study [[The Force/Legends|the Force]],<ref name="POJSB">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> a mystic energy that flowed through all living things and bound [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] together.<ref name="AGTSWU2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> The monks also discovered the [[Midi-chlorian/Legends|midi-chlorians]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> a [[Species/Legends|species]] of microscopic organisms that existed within the cells of every living creature and allowed them to touch the Force.<ref name="CSWE0">{{CSWECite|II|311|Midi-chlorian}}</ref> While other organizations had developed similar ideas elsewhere, including the [[Shaman/Legends|shamans]] of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] and the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] scholars from [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]],<ref name="DOJ1" /> they were isolated from each other and unable to share their views.<ref name="WDM" />

===First Migration===
[[File:Bendu_and_Tho_Yor-DOJ1.png|thumb|150px|The Bendu monks hearing the call of the Tho Yor]]
{{Quote|They heard the call in their hearts, in their minds. Not with words, yet as a voice.|Ketu|<ref name="DOJ1" />}}
Around [[37,453 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> the Dai Bendu monks discovered a mysterious pyramidal edifice half-buried in the snow of the Andobi Mountains. Intrigued by this wonder of unidentified origin, they called it the "[[Tho Yor]]" in their [[Dai Bendu language|language]]. While they understood that the Tho Yor came from another world, the monks could not guess its purpose or even see what was contained inside. Nonetheless, they felt a great power was contained in it. For many years, the monks gathered round the pyramid, kneeling before their object of adoration and [[Meditation/Legends|meditating]] even under heavy snowfalls.<ref name="DOJ1" />

After attending the edifice for a millennium, a prophecy was fulfilled when a group of monks heard the call of the Tho Yor in [[36,453 BBY]]. They entered it, discovering it was in fact a [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. The monks of the Dai Bendu were transported to the world of [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], in the galaxy's [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. Once there, they discovered that seven other Tho Yor had repeated the pattern elsewhere in the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], gathering [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] who could hear their call and bringing them to Tython. Together with other [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] organizations, the Bendu monks became the first [[Tythan]]s and contributed to the foundation of the [[Je'daii Order]], who sought to maintain peace through the harmony of balance.<ref name="DOJ1" /> In [[25,802 BBY]], a monk named [[Lha-Mi]] was the [[Je'daii Temple Master]] of [[Stav Kesh]], the Temple of Martial Arts.<ref name="ITV" /> The name ''Je'daii'' itself testified to the influence of Bendu philosophy on the Order. It was an amalgam of two Dai Bendu words: ''je'' meaning "mystic" and ''dai'' meaning "center".<ref name="DOJ0">[[Star Wars: Dawn of the Jedi 0|''Star Wars: Dawn of the Jedi'' 0]]</ref>

===Second migration===
[[File:Spires_of_Kalimahr.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The spires of Kalimahr]]
{{Quote|Beyond Tython, they settled first on Kalimahr, jewel of the Tython system, then Shikaakwa, the outlaw world, then on the forest planet Ska Gora, and on the moons of the giants—Obri and Mawr—and even the outermost planet, cold Furies Gate.|Ketu|<ref name="DOJ1" />}}
More than a millennium after the birth of the Je'daii Order, the Tythonian [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Aurum]] was destroyed, and the planet was deemed too dangerous for those not [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] to the Force. The non–Forceful Tythans thus started to [[Second migration|explore]] the [[Tython system/Legends|Tython system]] and establish [[Settled Worlds|colonies]] on the neighboring worlds.<ref name="DOJ1" /> The green-colored [[Kalimahr/Legends|Kalimahr]] was the first planet they settled, and the colonists had brought their religions with them. For that reason, Kalimahr became the spiritual hub of the system, teeming with religious communities and pilgrims. The Bendu monks had not been left behind and had built several temples on Kalimahr, [[Stargazers Temple|one]] of which was located in the eastern quarter of the [[Khar Peninsula]], a nineteen-[[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] long byland.<ref name="ITV" /> By [[25,793 BBY]], that particular building was abandoned by the Bendu monks and become a refuge for the [[Stargazers]], a cult-like group of fanatics who sought to reactivate an ancient [[hypergate]]. At that time, groups of Bendu monks could often be seen chanting outside the lobby of the [[Rhol Yan]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], with travelers settling around them and listening to their haunting chants.<ref name="ITV" />

===The Jedi and the Galactic Republic===
{{Quote|Xendor sought the hierarch's permission to create an academy for the study of Force traditions—to tap into the knowledge of the Chatos Academy and the Dai Bendu, the Palawa and the Kashi Mer, the Bogan and the Kel Dor sages, the Way of the Dark and Protectorate of the Hidden.|[[Arden Lyn]], [[Legions of Lettow|Legionnaire of Lettow]]|<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Back_to_back.png|thumb|200px|The Jedi Knights, spiritual heirs to the Bendu monks]]
Later on, the Je'daii Order was succeeded by one of its splinter groups, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]],<ref name="DOJ0" /> which ended up associating with the newly-formed [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] around [[24,953 BBY]].<ref name="T15">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Galactic History: The Jedi Join the Republic}}</ref> Through that new order of Force-users, some of the teachings and symbols that originated with the Dai Bendu were preserved for millennia. The Jedi notably endorsed the eight-rayed [[Republic crest/Legends|sigil]] of the Bendu monks, a symbol of the Force binding all living things in the galaxy,<ref name="CCS">''[[Star Wars: Complete Cross-Sections]]''</ref> having brandished that emblem during the [[Unification Wars (Galactic Republic)|Unification Wars]] that resulted in the founding of the Republic.<ref name="CSWE3">{{CSWECite|I|70|Bendu monks}}</ref> The Jedi renegade [[Xendor]] also drew on the Dai Bendu knowledge when he built his [[Legionnaire Academy|academy]] on planet [[Lettow]]. Ultimately, Xendor's controversial teachings led to the [[First Great Schism]] in the history of the Jedi.<ref name="EGW" />

At some point from [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]] to [[22,000 BBY/Legends|22,000 BBY]], the Order of Dai Bendu was present on [[Thape]], a celestial body in the Mid Rim.<ref name="TEA" /> At some point between [[3643 BBY|3643]]<ref name="Time Question">{{TORweb|url=community/showthread.php?p=1780110|text=Question To Bioware - 30 ATC Or Not?}}</ref> and [[3640 BBY]],<ref name="Encyclopedia">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> in the middle of the [[Galactic War]], a delegation of Bendu monks traveled throughout the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], escorting a casket that contained a meditating Bendu master. Opportunities to study Bendu artifact were rare, which prompted certain citizens of the Republic to approach the monks and ask for information.<ref name="SWTOR" />

===Fall into oblivion===
{{Quote|Welcome, podracing fans, to Ando Prime! Home of the benevolent Andobi Bendu monks!|A podracing announcer|<ref name="SWE1R">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']]</ref>}}
Many centuries flew past and, during the height of the Republic, the Order of Dai Bendu had been largely forgotten. Its name only appeared in a few crumbling history disks, and while several [[Historian/Legends|historians]] rightfully suspected that the Bendu monks were one of the original incarnations of the Jedi Order, they never found any evidence to support their claim.<ref name="POJSB" /> In his time, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]]—[[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]—was among the few individuals who possessed texts devoted to the Order of Dai Bendu and other early Force-centered groups such as the [[Followers of Palawa]] or the [[Chatos Academy]].<ref name="SW:DP">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> The fifth and last day of the [[Galactic Standard Calendar#Time Measurement|Galactic Standard week]] had also been named "[[Benduday/Legends|Benduday]]," sharing its name with the ancient monastic order.<ref name="HYP">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/dexdiner/index.html|text=Dining at Dex's|int=Dining at Dex's}}</ref>

[[File:TenAbuDonba-OPG.jpg|thumb|left|150px|High Priest Ten-Abu Donba, leader of the Dai Bendu around 32 BBY]]
Despite the general lack of academic knowledge about them, the Bendu monks still occupied a monastery in the Andobi Moutains around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Naboo Crisis]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], when they were led by the [[High Priest/Legends|High Priest]] [[Ten-Abu Donba]].<ref name="SWE1R" /> Far to the south of the monastery, modern cities had been established in recent decades. To suit the needs of modern life, a giant pump station and a large [[Andobi Mountain Pipeline|pipeline]] had been built to carry [[Water/Legends|water]] from its source, the lakes and glaciers of the Andobi Mountains. In order to entertain the thousands of laborers who worked on the cities, the pumping station and the pipeline, [[Podracing/Legends|podraces]] were held annually along the access roads around the mountain. Faithful to their non-confrontational beliefs, Donba's monks welcomed the various podracing events that wound their way through Ando Prime's icy terrain,<ref name="POSG">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> even though three of them tore right through their village, in the vicinity of the monk's [[Tent/Legends|tents]], in the shadow of a massive [[Stone/Legends|stone]] [[Bendu Statue|statue]]. The High Priest Donba himself served as the honorary starter for those races, under the warm cheers and acclamations of the public.<ref name="SWE1R" />

By [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], much had happened in the galaxy. The Republic had been replaced by the authoritarian [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which in turn had been toppled by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] after twenty-three years of tyranny. A [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] had been [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|founded]],<ref name="NEC" /> and a [[New Republic Centralized Archives|vast collection]] of documents designed to be exhaustive was created.<ref name="SWFF1">{{FactFile|1|[[:File:Galaxymap3.jpg|Galaxy Map Poster]]}}</ref> That Neo-Republican database, however, knew nothing of the Bendu monks' influence over the birth of the Jedi Order. Its files assumed that the details of the origins of the Jedi had been lost in time, speculating that the Order had emerged ''sua sponte'' at the same time as the original [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]]<ref name="FF75">{{FactFile|75|JED 1-4|The Jedi}}</ref> in [[25,053 BBY]].<ref name="Repubic">{{Holonet|allegiances|galactic-republic|The Galactic Republic}}</ref> By [[36 ABY]], the New Republic had been reorganized into the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] and their [[Historical Council]] had learned that the Order of Dai Bendu studied the Force before the formation of the Jedi Order.<ref name="NEC" />

==Organization and beliefs==
[[File:Bendu_Statue.png|thumb|250px|The Bendu Statue overlooking the tent village]]
The Dai Bendu monks of Ando Prime lived in a tribal community, away from the busy cities and under the guidance of the wisest of them all, who bore the title of High Priest.<ref name="CSWE1">{{CSWECite|I|197|Donba, Ten-Abu}}</ref> They dwelled in a simple tent [[Bendu Village|village]] nestled in the mountains and pure water, which they pumped from the Andobi Mountain Pipeline, was sacred to their beliefs—although they sometimes sold it to occasional visitors.<ref name="OPG" /> Those who had migrated to the Tython system had built temples of their own on the settled worlds, some of which were decorated with stained-glass symbols and frescoes relaying the monks' religious tales and history.<ref name="ITV" /> The monks also used to string rectangular pieces of cloth along mountain ridges, sometimes colorful,<ref name="SWE1R" /> sometimes covered with inscriptions.<ref name="DOJ1" />

They wore long, flowing [[Robe/Legends|robes]] of simple design, and some of them used walking poles to help them move in frozen terrains.<ref name="DOJ1" /> In the late years of the order, the High Priest donned elaborate orange<ref name="SWE1R" /> or red robes with a high, pointed hood.<ref name="OPG" /> The Bendu monks performed different kinds of chanting, some of which consisted in eerie and haunting ululations<ref name="ITV" /> while others required a low, deep voice, sounding like a growl.<ref name="SWE1Rsound">The soundtrack of the video game ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'' features that kind of low-voice chant at the beginning of the [[Podracing/Legends|podraces]] that occurred in the [[Andobi Mountains/Legends|Andobi Mountains]], land of the Bendu monks.</ref> Those who listened to their meditating voices would sway slowly in time with the chant.<ref name="ITV" />

[[File:RepublicBenduSymbol-AoI.png|thumb|left|110px|The Bendu sigil]]
The monks were known for teaching non-confrontational principles, with the notable exception of commercialism. In line with their professed commitment to peace, at the time of the Naboo Crisis, the monks not only hosted but also organized podraces,<ref name="OPG" /> a sport which was both notoriously dangerous and based on competition, though there is nothing confrontational or violent about competition and thus commercial capitalism is in line with their beliefs.<ref name="CSWE2">{{CSWECite|III|33|Podrace}}</ref> It notably allowed them to sell their crafts, including artisanal carpets, to the numerous podracing fans who visited Ando Prime.<ref name="OPG" /> The Bendu monks were still regarded as both dignified and kindly in the waning decades of the Galactic Republic.<ref name="SWE1R" />

Numerology, the study of the occult significance of numbers, was also used and developed by the Bendu monks. They believed that the number nine represented the beneficent presence of the Force in a unitary galaxy. At the time of the Unification Wars, they used to represent this through eight spokes joined to one disc.<ref name="ROTSICS">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref> That eight-spoked sigil was very similar, although not identical, to the one that was engraved on the Tho Yor's triangular faces.<ref name="DOJ1" /> The Bendu symbol was later reclaimed by the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]], who made it the emblem of his [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]].<ref name="TOR">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''</ref> Millennia later, the crest was reused once again. Renamed the "Galactic Roundel," the Bendu symbol adorned the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] of the Jedi Knights and saw use as the insignia of the Galactic Republic as a whole. After the fall of the Republic and its replacement by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the self-appointed [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine personalized this ancient icon by removing two spokes, thus creating the [[Imperial crest/Legends|Imperial crest]].<ref name="AOTCICS" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Origins===
{{Quote|Take my son! The Jedi-Bendu must survive. We must pass it on. Only a Jedi can stop the Empire. We're very old, Luke. A new generation of Jedi must be started. Take him; teach him the way of the Jedi-Bendu…|Kane Starkiller to General Luke Skywalker in "The Star Wars: Rough Draft"|<ref name="swrd">[[The Star Wars rough draft|''The Star Wars'' rough draft]]</ref>}}
The term "Jedi Bendu" was an early name that ''[[Star Wars]]'' creator [[George Lucas]] considered using for the Jedi in the [[1974]] [[The Star Wars rough draft|rough draft]] of what would become [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. In that script, the Jedi Bendu were described as "the most feared warriors in the universe" and "the personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] of the emperor."<ref name="roughdraft">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarz.com/starkiller/the-star-wars-rough-draft|text=THE STAR WARS by George Lucas - Rough Draft|work=Starkiller - The Jedi Bendu Script Site|archivedate=20191219100824}}</ref> The name of Bendu was later reused in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] of ''Star Wars'' to designate one of the religious organizations that gave rise to the Jedi Order.<ref name="NEC" />

===Development===
The concept of the Bendu being a primitive incarnation of the Jedi Order was first mentioned in the ''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'', which was written by [[J.D. Wiker]], [[Michael Mikaelian]], [[Jeff Grubb]], [[Owen K.C. Stephens]], and [[James Maliszewski]]<ref name="POJSB" /> and was published in [[August]] [[2002]].<ref name="Date2">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20020815cpreview&c=rpg|text=Take a Look Inside: A Preview of the ''Power of the Jedi Sourcebook''}}</ref> However, the monks themselves had made their first appearance in the [[1999]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'', although no connection with the Jedi had been established at the time.<ref name="SWE1R" /> Later reference books such as ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''<ref name="NEC" /> and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''<ref name="CSWE3" /> mentioned them without giving new details. It was not until [[2012]], with the release of the [[Star Wars: Dawn of the Jedi|''Dawn of the Jedi'']] series of comics, that the Dai Bendu monks appeared again in a ''Star Wars'' product, thirteen years after ''Star Wars: Episode I Racer''.<ref name="DOJ1" />

The Bendu monks received a brief mention in the [[2011]] [[Star Wars video games|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', released by [[BioWare]]. They were mentioned in the archaeology crew skill mission entitled "Secrets of the Bendu," available for players of the Republic faction only. In a crew skill mission, the player would send one of their companions to complete the quest. However, due to the mission being available to all classes, it was unknown which of the four Republic heroes—the [[Hero of Tython]], [[Voidhound]], the [[The Barsen'thor|Barsen'thor]] or the [[Meteor (Havoc Squad)|Havoc Squad commander]]—actually approached the Bendu monks. As a reward for the mission, the player received some polychromic [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]].<ref name="SWTOR" />

===Comparison with Buddhist traditions===
[[File:Dharma_wheel.svg|thumb|150px|The Dharmacakra, which the Bendu sigil resembles]]
The lifestyle and customs of the Bendu monks displays many similarities with [[Wikipedia:Buddhism|Buddhist]] [[Wikipedia:Buddhist monasticism|monasticism]]. Like the Tibetan monks,<ref name="MUM">{{WebCite|url=https://www.travelmag.co.uk/2005/05/buddhism-at-its-purest-ladakhs-mountain-monks|text=Buddhism at its purest: Ladakh's mountain monks|work=Travelmag|archivedate=20201021202421}}</ref> the Dai Bendu dwell in the snowy mountains.<ref name="DOJ1" /> In addition, the clothes worn by High Priest Ten-Abu Donba resemble<ref name="SWE1R" /> the red robes and pointed hats worn by the "Red Sect" of Tibetan Buddhism.<ref name="RED">{{WebCite|url=http://www.thranguhk.org/buddhism/en_buddhism.html?keepThis=tr|text=Buddhism in Tibet|work=Tranguhk.org|archivedate=20201021222208}}</ref> Both ''Episode I Racer''<ref name="SWE1R" /> and the ''Dawn of the Jedi'' comics<ref name="DOJ1" /> showed that the Bendu monks made [[Wikipedia:Prayer flag|prayer flags]] similar to those used in Tibetan Buddhism.<ref name="OXF">{{WebCite|url=http://www.thepeaceflagproject.org/historyoftibetanflags.htm|text=The Tradition of Tibetan Prayer Flags|work=The Peace Flag Project|archivedate=20201021222111}}</ref>

In some [[Wikipedia:Buddhism|Buddhist]] traditions, mummified bodies of monks are displayed in a meditating [[Wikipedia:Lotus position|lotus posture]] inside an upright coffin.<ref name="MUM2">{{WebCite|url=http://www.thaibuddhist.com/the-mummified-monk-in-samui|text=The Mummified Monk in Samui|work=Buddhism in Thailand|archivedate=20190429033300}}</ref> The "Secrets of the Bendu" crew skill quest that appeared in ''Star Wars: The Old Republic'' showed that the same practice existed among the Dai Bendu.<ref name="SWTOR">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Archaeology|Secrets of the Bendu}}</ref> The eight-spoked sigil of the Dai Bendu also resembles<ref name="AOTCICS" /> the [[Wikipedia:Dharmacakra|Dharmacakra]] or "Wheel of the Law," a symbol of the [[Wikipedia:Gautama Buddha|Buddha's]] teachings.<ref name="OXF 2">{{WebCite|url=http://www.oxfordreference.com/view/10.1093/oi/authority.20110803095715193|text=Dharmacakra|work=Oxford Reference|archiveurl=https://archive.vn/wj4Q7}}</ref>
